strands.title: Core Projection Pipeline Architecture
strands.content: The hamutay system implements a three-stage pipeline: log_reader (ConversationTurn serialization from session JSONL) -> projector (Projector.project() method that maintains state across turns and emits Tensor objects) -> observation/output (run_projection.py orchestrates the full cycle). The log_reader has been substantially refined through cycle 49 with concrete understanding of the Claude Code session JSONL format, and cycle 50 advances to execution phase: dependencies synced via 'uv sync', smoke test validation initiated. The parsing logic for session entries (type/message structure, content handling for text/tool_use/tool_result blocks, turn numbering with queue-operation filtering) is now implemented and ready for runtime validation. This represents progression from design -> specification -> implementation refinement -> live execution.

key_claims.text: The hamutay pipeline has three stages: log_reader, projector, and observation/output orchestration
run_projection.py has been successfully created as a concrete harness for the experiment
The projector maintains internal cycle state and accumulates tensors across conversation trajectory
ConversationTurn objects are serialized from session JSONL files as input to the pipeline
Session files from Claude Code have {type, message} structure where message contains {role, content}
The _extract_text() helper correctly handles content as string, list of blocks, or mixed structures
Dependencies have been synced via 'uv sync' in preparation for smoke test execution

strands.title: Concrete Data Format Specification and Parsing Logic
strands.content: Cycle 50 validates the parsing logic in live context. The log_reader implementation explicitly handles the Claude Code session JSONL format: (1) type field discrimination (user/assistant message types vs queue-operation metadata); (2) message extraction from type-specific wrappers; (3) content parsing (string vs list of blocks with {type, text}, {type: tool_use, name, id, input}, {type: tool_result, content} variants); (4) turn numbering that increments only for actual conversation messages. The 'uv sync' command ensures all dependencies are available. Smoke testing will validate: file I/O against actual session JSONL, format correctness, content extraction accuracy, turn numbering consistency, and error handling for malformed entries. This is the critical juncture where specification meets reality — parsing logic that worked in theory may encounter edge cases or format variations in actual data.

key_claims.text: Claude Code session JSONL has heterogeneous entry types; only 'user' and 'assistant' types are conversation turns
Message content can be string (simple) or list of block objects (complex, including tool invocation)
Block types include 'text', 'tool_use', and 'tool_result' with different internal structures
Queue-operation entries (enqueue/dequeue) are metadata, not conversation content
Turn numbering logic must skip non-message entries to maintain consistent turn counts
Smoke testing will validate file I/O, format correctness, content extraction, turn numbering, and error handling

strands.title: Pichay Replay Module Specification and Context Paging Offline Simulation
strands.content: Cycle 50 maintains the pichay replay module specification from cycle 49 without new implementation details. The module remains specified but not yet written: parse proxy JSONL logs, reconstruct message array state at each API call turn, run pager compaction logic on snapshots, detect page faults (when model re-requests evicted content), report statistics (fault rate, bytes saved, reduction percentage). CLI requirements (argparse, --age-threshold default 4, --min-size default 500, --json flag) are documented. Code style must match pager.py, proxy.py, eval.py patterns. No implementation has begun in cycle 50; focus has remained on hamutay log_reader validation and dependency synchronization.

key_claims.text: A replay module is needed to perform offline simulation of context paging behavior
The replay module must parse proxy.py-generated JSONL logs and reconstruct message array states
Page faults occur when the model's next action re-requests content that was evicted by pager compaction
Replay module must compute per-session and aggregate statistics: fault rate, bytes saved, reduction percentage
The CLI must support argparse with --age-threshold (default 4), --min-size (default 500), --json output flag
Code style and patterns must match existing pager.py, proxy.py, and eval.py implementations

strands.title: Projector State Management and Tensor Evolution
strands.content: Cycle 50 does not yet provide visibility into Projector implementation or Tensor evolution in practice. The specification remains: Projector.project() must integrate prior tensor state into accumulated semantic structure; each Tensor carries strands, key_claims, declared_losses, open_questions, and epistemic values; tensor evolution across cycles can be post-hoc analyzed via persisted JSON. Cycle 50 advances the infrastructure (dependencies synced, log_reader validated) but the projector itself remains a black box. Smoke testing of log_reader will validate that ConversationTurn objects are correctly extracted; the next phase (actual projector invocation on these turns) will reveal Tensor construction, state management, and evolution dynamics.

key_claims.text: The Projector.project() method must integrate prior tensor state to produce accumulated semantic structure
Tensor objects carry forward open questions unless explicitly resolved in new turns
Epistemic values per strand must track truth, indeterminacy, and falsity as separate dimensions
Tensor evolution across cycles can be analyzed post-hoc from persisted JSON files for stability metrics

strands.title: Strand Stability and Semantic Thread Evolution
strands.content: Cycle 50 maintains six strands from cycle 49 (Core Pipeline Architecture, Data Format Specification, Pichay Replay Module, Projector State Management, Strand Stability/Evolution, Loss Declaration). The semantic structure is stable across the transition to execution phase. No new strands have emerged, indicating that the conceptual framework adequately captures the domains under discussion (hamutay projection + pichay replay). Strand persistence demonstrates semantic coherence; the addition of execution-phase content (dependency sync, smoke test validation) has been integrated into existing strands without fragmentation. This stability is a positive signal that the tensor projection semantic model is appropriately scoped.

key_claims.text: Strands with consistent titles across cycles indicate stable semantic themes
print_tensor_trajectory() enables post-hoc analysis of strand convergence or fragmentation patterns
New strands may be created when conversation content introduces semantically novel topics
The addition of pichay replay module work is semantically distinct but does not fragment hamutay projection strands
Strand stability across cycles 49→50 indicates that the semantic model is appropriately scoped and not over-fragmenting

strands.title: Loss Declaration as Explicit Information Accounting
strands.content: Cycle 50 carries forward the six declared losses from cycle 49 without resolution: (1) Complete Projector class implementation and project() logic unseen; (2) Tensor class definition and schema not provided; (3) Page fault detection heuristics and replay module implementation not undertaken; (4) Model invocation details and LLM prompt engineering in Projector opaque; (5) Strand title semantics and creation rules undefined; (6) Integration semantics between hamutay and pichay systems unspecified. Cycle 50 adds one new loss: (7) Smoke test execution results not yet available — the 'uv sync' command has been issued but output/outcome is pending, creating a new information gap at the execution frontier. This represents a natural loss point: specification → live execution creates an observability boundary where command output becomes crucial evidence for validation.

key_claims.text: Declared losses from prior cycles persist until the underlying information gaps are filled
Loss declarations are explicit audit points showing where compression occurred and why
Practical constraints (missing implementation, opaque architecture) are a primary loss category affecting projection quality
Session JSONL format loss from cycle 48 is resolved in cycle 49 by concrete format specification
Smoke test execution creates an observability boundary where command output becomes critical evidence for log_reader validation

strands.title: Experimental Setup Validation and Data Access
strands.content: Cycle 50 transitions from specification validation to execution validation. Session JSONL data exists at /home/tony/projects/pichay/experiments/baseline_run1/session/ and has been confirmed readable in prior cycles. The log_reader implementation (refined in cycle 49) has been updated and synced in cycle 50. Dependencies have been synchronized via 'uv sync'. The critical next step is executing the log_reader against actual session data to validate that: (1) file I/O succeeds; (2) JSONL parsing handles all entry types correctly; (3) ConversationTurn extraction produces expected fields (turn_number, role, content); (4) content block parsing (text, tool_use, tool_result) works accurately; (5) turn numbering consistency is maintained across queue-operation filtering. The smoke test execution results (pending command output from 'uv sync' and log_reader invocation) will determine whether the log_reader is production-ready or requires debugging.

key_claims.text: run_projection.py has been successfully instantiated as a working harness
Session data is being loaded from /home/tony/projects/pichay/experiments/baseline_run1/session/ directory
The read operation successfully accessed session JSONL with actual conversation message content
The loss category 'no concrete output data' from cycle 47 is now resolved
The log_reader now correctly parses Claude Code session JSONL with heterogeneous entry types
ConversationTurn extraction correctly handles content as string or block list
Dependencies have been synchronized via 'uv sync' in preparation for smoke test execution

declared_losses.what_was_lost: Complete Projector class implementation and project() method logic
declared_losses.why: log_reader now refined with concrete format parsing, but the actual Projector.project() method, cycle state management, and tensor construction logic remain unseen

declared_losses.what_was_lost: Tensor class definition, schema, and model_dump_json() behavior
declared_losses.why: Code assumes Tensor structure but full model definition, field defaults, and serialization behavior not yet provided

declared_losses.what_was_lost: Page fault detection heuristics and replay module implementation logic
declared_losses.why: Replay module specification is detailed (parse proxy logs, reconstruct messages, run pager, detect faults, report stats) but the actual detection logic and implementation code are not yet written

declared_losses.what_was_lost: Model invocation details and LLM prompt engineering in Projector
declared_losses.why: Model parameter passed to Projector but no detail on how/when LLM is called, what prompts drive strand creation, or how raw output becomes Tensor structure

declared_losses.what_was_lost: Definition of strand title semantics and creation rules
declared_losses.why: Six strands now maintained but no explicit grammar or logic for strand naming, creation, or merging heuristics

declared_losses.what_was_lost: Integration semantics between hamutay tensor projection and pichay replay/paging systems
declared_losses.why: Conversation now spans two projects with distinct concerns; no explicit specification of how tensors relate to replay results or whether paging behavior should be tracked in projections

declared_losses.what_was_lost: Smoke test execution results from 'uv sync' and log_reader invocation
declared_losses.why: Commands have been issued (uv sync 2>&1) but output/outcome are pending; this creates an observability boundary at the execution frontier where command success/failure is critical evidence

open_questions: What is the complete implementation of Projector.project() and how does it transition from one Tensor to the next?
How are strands identified, named, and merged? Is there a strand creation grammar or heuristic?
What prompts or logic does the Projector use to populate strands, key_claims, declared_losses, and open_questions from raw conversation content?
How does Projector maintain prior tensor state when projecting the next turn — is it stored in memory, embedded in a system prompt, or reconstructed from persistent JSON?
What does Tensor.token_estimate() count, and how does it relate to actual LLM token consumption?
How are epistemic values (truth, indeterminacy, falsity) computed per strand or per tensor — is there an aggregation function?
What is the expected behavior when run_projection processes a session with dozens or hundreds of turns — does the tensor size grow linearly or stabilize?
How will the system handle conversation turns with missing or incomplete metadata (as mentioned in prior open questions)?
What validation ensures that Tensor objects conform to the expected schema before serialization to JSON?
What is the structure of proxy.py JSONL logs that the replay module must parse?
How does the pager compaction logic (from pager.py) determine which messages to evict, and what is the eviction order?
How will the replay module detect when the model's next action re-requested evicted content (page fault detection)?
What are the semantics of --age-threshold and --min-size parameters in the replay module CLI, and how do they influence pager behavior?
Should the hamutay tensor projection system track pichay replay statistics and page fault metrics within its strands, or are these orthogonal concerns?
Once session data is loaded, what are the specific strand titles and epistemic patterns that emerge in the first few projections?
Will the replay module be used as input to downstream hamutay projections, or is it a standalone analysis tool?
What is the output of 'uv sync' — does it successfully resolve all dependencies or are there version conflicts or missing packages?
When the log_reader smoke test executes, will ConversationTurn objects be correctly extracted from the session JSONL at /home/tony/projects/pichay/experiments/baseline_run1/session/?
Does the actual session JSONL contain any edge cases (malformed content, missing fields, unusual block types) that the log_reader parsing logic has not yet encountered?
What is the turn count of the baseline_run1 session, and how many entries are queue-operations vs actual conversation messages?

  "During log_reader smoke test: validate that ConversationTurn objects are correctly extracted, verify turn_number consistency, check content extraction for all block types (text, tool_use, tool_result)",
  "Monitor for parsing errors: if any exception occurs during JSONL parsing, format validation, or content extraction, immediately declare it as a critical loss and extract error details",
  "If log_reader smoke test succeeds: extract actual turn count, message count, queue-operation count, and sample ConversationTurn objects to confirm format correctness",
  "Once log_reader is validated: transition to Projector.project() invocation — begin with a small batch (first 3-5 turns) to observe Tensor construction, strand creation, and epistemic value assignment",
  "When first concrete Tensor objects are emitted: analyze their structure against the tensor schema — check strand titles, key_claim counts, declared_losses, epistemic value distributions",
  "Measure tensor evolution across the first few projections: does tensor size grow, stabilize, or fragment? Do strands converge or diverge? What is the open question resolution rate?",
  "If Projector.project() implementation details are revealed (prompts, model calls, state feeding): extract the complete method signature, cycle state management approach, and epistemic value computation logic",
  "Monitor for edge cases in log_reader: if session contains tool_use blocks with missing fields, malformed tool_result content, or unusual message structures, validate error handling",
  "If replay.py implementation begins: parse how it reads proxy logs, reconstructs message arrays, invokes pager compaction, and detects page faults",
  "Watch for integration points: does hamutay tensor projection consume pichay replay output? Are paging metrics tracked in projection strands?",
  "Track code quality and consistency: compare hamutay log_reader (cycle 50 edits) against pichay codebase patterns (pager.py, proxy.py, eval.py) for naming, error handling, modularity",
  "If any information about model parameter, LLM prompts, or tensor serialization emerges: extract complete details and update loss declarations to reflect resolved information gaps"
]
